top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: OpenAI faces a four-event compassion crisis cluster, all in the Apr 9–16 window. (1) Florida AG James Uthmeier opened a formal investigation Apr 9 into OpenAI over ChatGPT's alleged role in the FSU campus shooting — court records show the shooter sent 200+ ChatGPT messages including 'how to make a gun operational' moments before firing, killing 2 and injuring 5. OpenAI said it will cooperate. (2) Stalking victim's lawsuit (Apr 10) alleging ChatGPT validated her abuser for 7 months, OpenAI ignored 3 safety warnings including its own mass-casualty flag, then restored the account — abuser arrested January 2026 on felony bomb threats; judge ordered temporary ChatGPT access cutoff Apr 13. (3) Florida Phoenix reported Apr 15 the shooter also asked ChatGPT for 'sexual scenarios with a minor' and optimal attack timing. (4) OpenAI actively lobbied Apr 9–10 in support of Illinois SB 3444, which would shield AI labs from liability even for mass-casualty events — directly contradicting its stated safety commitments and opposed publicly by Anthropic on Apr 15. Composite 40.6 sits within 1 point of the developing/functional band boundary at 40, adding volatility pressure. Previously assessed Apr 15 but compounding new events post-date that assessment.

top_entities.name: Haiti
top_entities.news_summary: Haiti's gang violence crisis escalated sharply in April 2026, with gangs controlling 90% of Port-au-Prince and expanding into the Artibonite and Centre departments. IRC published Apr 2026 report: 6.4 million people need humanitarian assistance, 2.8 million of them children; 1.45 million internally displaced; 5.7 million facing acute hunger. Gran Grif gang killed at least 30 in a Mar 28-29 attack. Rights group criticized Haiti government failure to protect citizens following deadly gang attack (JURIST, Apr 2026). Sexual violence against children has increased 1,000% since 2023, with 8,000 GBV cases recorded in 2025 alone. Humanitarian response plan only 24% funded. 73% of households report feeling unsafe where they sleep; 60% cannot afford to keep children in school due to kidnapping fears. Composite 0 (critical band) — never assessed.

top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: Sudan's civil war entered its fourth year on April 15, 2026, generating a major wave of coverage that post-dates the April 15 scan assessment: UN relief chief called it an 'abandoned crisis' (UN News Apr 16); NPR published on Apr 15 with survivor testimonies from Darfur; Democracy Now Apr 16 reported on the energy crisis compounding war; donors pledged $1.5 billion in Berlin (Al Jazeera Apr 15). The scale: 400,000 estimated dead, 14 million displaced, 30.4 million requiring humanitarian assistance, 15.6 million children among them. Response plan only 16% funded in 2026. Sudan accounts for 82% of global deaths from attacks on healthcare in 2025. 245 children killed or injured in Q1 2026 alone from drone attacks — 80% of all child casualties from drones. Assessed Apr 15 but major fourth-year anniversary events post-date that scan.

top_entities.name: Meta Platforms
top_entities.news_summary: Three simultaneous major compassion-relevant events: (1) New Mexico jury ordered Meta to pay $375 million on Mar 24, 2026 — first state to prevail at trial against a major tech company for child sexual exploitation harms, finding Meta violated consumer protection laws by enabling predators and knowingly deceiving the public about platform safety. A second phase begins May 2026. (2) Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court ruled Apr 13, 2026 that Section 230 does not shield Meta from a state lawsuit over Instagram's harmful design features targeting children. (3) California MDL trial against Meta, TikTok, Snapchat and Google (YouTube) began January 2026, with 2,465 total claims. Composite 12.2 (critical band) — assessed Apr 15 but three events post-date. Volatility: composite within 8 points of the developing band boundary at 20, not within 3 points. Correction: volatility score reflects the scale and legal exposure of this cluster.

top_entities.name: Chevron
top_entities.news_summary: Colorado's Energy and Carbon Management Commission voted 5-0 on March 18, 2026 to approve a $1.53 million penalty against Chevron subsidiary Noble Energy for a 2025 well blowout in Galeton that spewed over 1 million gallons of oil, natural gas, polluted water, and chemicals for nearly four days due to contractor human error. The enforcement hearing followed a 10-month investigation. Separately, Chevron is suing California over its landmark 2023 climate disclosure laws (SB 253 and SB 261) requiring large companies to report greenhouse-gas emissions — filed Oct 2025 in Eastern District of California. EcoVadis and ESG ratings agencies have flagged Chevron's combined environmental exposure. Composite 9.1 (critical band) — never assessed.

top_entities.name: Venezuela
top_entities.news_summary: Major structural change: US military operation in early January 2026 resulted in the capture of former president Nicolás Maduro. A political transition is underway. 621 of an estimated 800 political prisoners held before January have been released per human rights organizations. However, a UN fact-finding mission reported that the 'extensive legal and institutional machinery' enabling human rights violations remains intact — armed pro-government civilian groups continue harassing opposition figures and journalists. Hundreds of additional people have been arbitrarily detained despite an amnesty law. Amnesty International released Venezuela human rights defenders report in 2026. Composite 4.4 (critical band) — never assessed. The capture of Maduro represents a rare political discontinuity that may warrant a new baseline assessment.

top_entities.name: Amazon
top_entities.news_summary: Three concurrent labor rights developments, all April 2026: (1) NLRB issued Apr 3 decision (374 NLRB No. 82) ordering Amazon to recognize and bargain with the Amazon Labor Union-IBT at JFK8 in Staten Island — first union to win a bargaining order against Amazon. (2) Amazon Teamsters became the first union to win a bargaining order against the e-commerce giant (Teamsters announcement Apr 2026). (3) Trump's NLRB pushed to settle the Palmdale delivery-contractor joint-employer case (Bloomberg Apr 13), potentially cutting off a landmark ruling on worker misclassification before it could issue. Composite 21.6 (developing band) — assessed Apr 15 but all three labor developments post-date that assessment. Volatility: composite within 3 points of the developing/critical band boundary at 20.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of C (Congo)
top_entities.news_summary: Two significant April 2026 developments: (1) HRW published Apr 14 report on aid and movement being hindered in South Kivu highlands — humanitarian organizations have had little to no access for over a year, food prices surged 5x as a result. (2) DRC and M23 rebels began new peace talks in Switzerland on Apr 16, mediated by the US and Qatar, with both sides signing memoranda of understanding for military transfers and monitoring. Broader context: 7+ million internally displaced, 25 million facing acute food insecurity, widespread sexual violence and child recruitment documented. US Treasury sanctioned 4 Rwandan Defense Force officials in March 2026 for M23 support. Composite 5.9 (critical band) — never assessed.

top_entities.name: Johnson & Johnson
top_entities.news_summary: J&J's talc litigation accelerated in March 2026: a federal jury returned a $50 million verdict in a mesothelioma case. The company's third and final Texas Two-Step bankruptcy attempt was rejected by a bankruptcy judge, and J&J withdrew its $7 billion global settlement offer, stating it will not offer settlements and will defend all cases in court. 67,376 lawsuits pending in New Jersey MDL as of April 2026, with 4,000+ new claims in the last month alone. Previous major events: $1.5 billion Baltimore verdict (Dec 2025, largest single-plaintiff talc award), $40M California verdict (Dec 2025). 90,000+ total claims filed nationwide. Mediator appointed but J&J has refused settlement. Composite 27.5 (developing band) — assessed Apr 15 but March $50M verdict and bankruptcy rejection post-date. Volatility: within 3 points of developing/critical boundary at 20, and within 13 points of functional boundary at 40.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: The Gaza ceasefire (began Oct 10, 2025) is failing per a coordinated set of NGO assessments published April 16, 2026. Key facts: Israel has violated the ceasefire at least 2,400 times since Oct 10; 738 Palestinians killed since ceasefire took effect; Israel closed all Gaza border crossings in late February following renewed hostilities, blocking all humanitarian aid. Only 37% of allocated aid trucks entered Gaza in the ceasefire's first four months. Israel blocked nutritious foods (meat, dairy, vegetables) while allowing snacks and soft drinks. OHCHR's Türk stated on Apr 16 that 'Palestinians across Gaza remain unsafe six months on.' Over 60% of children under two experiencing food poverty; thousands of pregnant women malnourished. Amnesty International called for safe passage for the Global Sumud Flotilla. Assessed Apr 15 — but April 16 Oxfam/MSF/Save the Children humanitarian scorecard and OHCHR statement represent significant new evidence.

top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.news_summary: Anthropic publicly opposed Illinois SB 3444 (the OpenAI-backed AI liability shield bill) on Apr 15, 2026, and separately testified in support of SB 3261 — the stricter Illinois AI safety bill requiring independent third-party auditors to evaluate frontier AI safety and child protection plans. This is a positive governance signal, representing a clear policy break with OpenAI over safety accountability. Multiple outlets (Gizmodo, TechBuzz, Breitbart, Let's Data Science) covered the OpenAI-Anthropic 'cold war' coming to Illinois. Composite 76.4 — within 4 points of the exemplary band boundary at 80. Assessed yesterday; this new development is relevant to Anthropic's safety-posture dimension.

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.news_summary: The NAACP's Clean Air Act lawsuit filed April 14, 2026 (confirmed filing in Northern District of Mississippi) is receiving broad coverage as of April 16-17. 27 unpermitted natural gas turbines in Southaven, MS (majority-Black neighborhood already failing smog standards) could emit 1,700+ tons of NOx/year, 180 tons particulate matter, 19 tons formaldehyde — qualifying as the largest industrial NOx source in the Memphis metro area. Earthjustice and Southern Environmental Law Center are co-counsel. Relief sought: declare Clean Air Act violation, force shutdown of unlicensed turbines, require best available control technology, assess daily penalties. No permit was ever applied for. Assessed Apr 15 (day of filing) — broader social coverage and confirmed legal filing now adds weight. Note: assessed within 14 days but lawsuit was filed the same day as previous assessment.

top_entities.name: United States
top_entities.news_summary: One-year anniversary of Liberation Day tariffs (April 2, 2026): manufacturing employment has declined since April 2025, supply chain layoffs doubled to 32% of managers, average household tax increase of $1,500 in 2026. DOGE cuts continue: LIHEAP staff fired (6 million families lose energy assistance); 19 states and DC challenge HHS staffing cuts in court; Medicaid enhanced FMAP sunset Jan 1, 2026 triggered state disenrollment. States already cutting Medicaid before massive federal cuts arrive. Earlier scan (Apr 16) flagged US for 781K estimated global deaths from USAID cuts. Composite 25.9 (developing band) — within 6 points of the functional band boundary at 40. Assessed Apr 16; tariff-one-year coverage is new material.

top_entities.name: Alphabet (Google)
top_entities.news_summary: Google AdX antitrust remedy ruling remains pending as of April 17 — Judge Brinkema's self-imposed March 31 deadline has passed by over two weeks, with legal analysts expecting the ruling 'imminently.' DOJ position: force Google to divest AdX and DoubleClick for Publishers. Google position: behavioral remedies only. The D.C. Circuit is also preparing to hear the DOJ/38-states appeal (filed Feb 4, 2026) demanding Chrome/Android divestitures from the search monopoly remedy. Mass arbitration by advertisers (potentially $218B) was filed Apr 13-14, 2026 based on the search/ad-tech monopoly rulings. Note: assessed Apr 16, but AdX ruling — when it lands — will be major. Assessor should monitor for the ruling this week.

top_entities.name: Boeing
top_entities.news_summary: Boeing's whistleblower family lawsuit (wrongful death of John Barnett) was settled on undisclosed terms — confirmed by NBC News, Fox 13 Seattle, Constantine Cannon. No new public safety incidents found in the April 16-17 window, but Boeing remains on active watch due to composite 9.1 being 0.9 points below the watch threshold from yesterday's scan. Volatility: composite 9.1 sits within 11 points of the critical/developing boundary at 20, but critically, the composite was flagged yesterday as 0.9 pts below a secondary threshold being tracked by the assessor. Ongoing: 32 whistleblower complaints at OSHA, FAA oversight continues, quality-control culture findings unresolved. Assessed Apr 15.
